perspectives.description: The clinical researcher is responsible for making ethical decisions regarding participant consent and the integrity of the study. They must balance the need for data with the ethical obligation to ensure valid consent.

The long-term participant is directly affected by the decision regarding their continued involvement in the study. Their fluctuating capacity to consent raises concerns about their autonomy and well-being.
Future patients with the same neurodegenerative condition may benefit from the research findings generated by the study. Their well-being relies on the integrity and continuity of the research data.
